title: Visualizing Higher-Order Transition Probabilities in a Markov Chain
description: A state graph showing how to compute the probability of transitioning from state A to state B in exactly n steps by summing the probabilities of all possible paths.
relationships: Paths from A to B: A->C->B (P(AC)*P(CB)), A->D->B (P(AD)*P(DB)), A->A->B (P(AA)*P(AB))
The n-step probability is the sum of probabilities for all paths of length n from A to B
This demonstrates the (A,B) entry of the transition matrix raised to the nth power (P^n)
